info.title: AgentOS API
info.description:
REST + WebSocket control-plane API for AgentOS.

**Response envelope:** all success responses are wrapped in `{ "data": ... }`; paginated list endpoints also include a `{ "meta": { "total": N } }` block. Errors use `{ "error": { "code", "message", "status" } }`.

**Auth:** send `Authorization: Bearer agos_<key>` on protected routes. The WebSocket endpoint authenticates via a `?token=agos_<key>` query parameter.

The OpenAI-compatible `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` returns its native shape (not the `{ data }` envelope) and streams `text/event-stream` chunks when `stream` is true.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/agent-chats` — Create a conversation and start its orchestration
loop in the background. Returns the created conversation; poll `GET {id}` for
turns + status (`active` → `complete`/`stopped`/`error`).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/agent-chats/{id}` — Get a conversation with its turn timeline.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/agent-chats/{id}/stop` — Stop a running conversation after its
current turn.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/agents/{name}` — Get detailed info for a single agent.

delete.description:
We look up the agent ID from the name via `get_agent_detail`, then call
`disconnect_agent`.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/agents/{name}/permissions` — Grant a permission to an agent.
`POST /api/v1/agents/{name}/settings` — Update editable settings for an agent.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/audit/logs/{trace_id}` — Get a specific audit entry by trace ID.

get.description:
Returns 501 Not Implemented until full tamper detection (chained hashes
across the append-only log) is wired.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/auth/login` — Exchange the operator credential for a scoped,
expiring API key. Public, but heavily rate-limited to resist brute force.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/auth/me` — Identity and scopes of the presented key.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` — Rotate the presented key: mint a fresh key with
the same scopes and a new TTL, and revoke the old one. Gated by
`[api] refresh_enabled` (the route is absent when disabled).

post.description:
NOTE: This endpoint follows the OpenAI response format for client library
compatibility and does NOT use the standard `{ "data": ... }` envelope.

When `stream: true`, returns an SSE stream of OpenAI-format chunks.

200.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion. When request `stream` is true, responds with text/event-stream SSE chunks instead of this JSON body.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}` — Get a session with its message timeline.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}` — Delete a session and its messages.

patch.summary: `PATCH /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}` — Rename a session (or clear the title).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}/export` — Export a session as JSON or markdown.
get.description:
Returns raw bytes (NOT the `{ data }` envelope) with a download-safe
`Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition`. `?format=json` (default) or
`?format=markdown`.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}/fork` — Fork a session into a new copy.

`POST /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}/messages` — send a user message and get the
assistant reply (non-streaming). Both turns are persisted to the session.

`POST /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}/messages/stream` — send a user message and
stream the assistant reply as Server-Sent Events (`thinking`/`chunk`/
`tool_start`/`tool_result`/`done`/`error`). Both turns are persisted.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/config` — Full config tree with secret-bearing leaves redacted.
`GET /api/v1/config/{key}` — Resolve a dotted config key from the live file.

put.summary:
`PUT /api/v1/config/{key}` — Write a dotted config key (gated by
`[api] config_writable`; returns 403 when disabled). The running kernel's
`ConfigWatcher` auto-reloads after the write.

403.description: Config writes disabled or insufficient scope

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/connectors` — List registered connectors and connection status.
`GET /api/v1/costs/agents/{name}` — Get cost summary for a specific agent.
`GET /api/v1/costs/summary` — Get cost summary across all agents.

`GET /api/v1/dashboard` — Composite dashboard summary (agents, task counts,
tool count, uptime, recent audit, background tasks).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/doctor/fix` — Attempt to auto-repair failing checks, then
re-run all checks and return the updated report.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/escalations` — List escalations (pending by default, or all).

parameters.name: pending
parameters.description:
When `true`, return only pending (unresolved) escalations. When `false`
or omitted, return all escalations (including resolved).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/escalations/{id}` — Get a single escalation by numeric ID.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/escalations/{id}/resolve` — Resolve an escalation with a decision.
post.description: Returns 409 Conflict if the escalation is already resolved or expired.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/events/emit` — Emit an event into the kernel event bus.

get.description:
Each event is emitted as an SSE frame whose `event:` is the event name and
`data:` is the JSON payload. The connection is kept alive with periodic
comments. Mirrors the WebSocket `subscribe` flow over a one-way stream.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description:
Channel to subscribe to (`tasks`, `agents`, `audit`, `schedules`,
`system`, or `events`). Subscribing requires the matching `<channel>:r`
scope on the API key.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/files` — List files for the authenticated principal.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Post-filter to files carrying this tag (FileStore has no native tag filter).

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Restrict to a scope (`"global"` or `"session:{id}"`). Omit for all scopes.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/files/{id}` — Remove a file record and its bytes from disk.

get.description:
This route returns raw bytes (NOT the `{ data }` envelope) with a safe
`Content-Type` (allowlisted via the kernel) and a header-safe
`Content-Disposition`.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/hal` — Hardware abstraction layer device inventory + snapshot.

`GET /api/v1/keys` — List all API keys with management metadata (never the
raw key or hash).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/keys` — Mint a new scoped API key. The raw key is returned once.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/keys/{id}` — Revoke a key by its public id. Idempotent-ish:
returns 404 if no key with that id exists. Keys are never hard-deleted.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/logs` — Query the audit log with optional level/since filters.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: RFC3339 lower bound on the entry timestamp.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of lines to return (default 200).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/marketplace?q=&type=` — Search the registry (empty list on failure).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/marketplace/{name}/reviews` — Submit a review to the registry.
`POST /api/v1/mcp/{name}/detach` — Stop and remove an MCP server.
`POST /api/v1/pipelines/import` — Install a pipeline from raw YAML.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/pipelines/{name}/export` — Export a pipeline as raw YAML.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Proposal status to filter by: `pending` (default), `accepted`, or `rejected`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of proposals to return (default 50).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/resources` — Host memory/disk snapshot plus live resource locks.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/roles` — Create a new role with permissions.
post.description: Returns 409 Conflict if a role with the same name already exists.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/roles/{name}` — Get a single role by name.

`GET /api/v1/schedules` — List all scheduled entries: recurring cron
schedules plus agent-created one-shot once-jobs and timers (see `kind`).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/schedules/preview` — Compute upcoming fire times for a cron
expression without creating anything. Pure; no kernel state is touched.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/schedules/{id}/runs` — List recorded fires of a schedule.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of runs to return (default 50, capped at 500).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/scratchpad` — List pages in the global scratchpad.
`GET /api/v1/scratchpad/{page}` — Read a page from the global scratchpad.

put.summary: `PUT /api/v1/scratchpad/{page}` — Create or overwrite a global page.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/secrets` — List all secrets (metadata only, no values).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/tasks/run` — Submit a new task for execution.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/tasks/{id}` — Get a single task by ID.
`GET /api/v1/tasks/{id}/checkpoints` — List checkpoints for a task (0 or 1).

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/resume` — Resume a task from its latest checkpoint.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/tasks/{id}/trace` — Get execution trace for a task.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/tools` — Install a tool from a manifest path.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/tools/{name}` — Get a specific tool by name.
get.description:
Note: The `KernelService` trait does not have a `get_tool` method, so we
filter the full list. This is acceptable for the current tool count.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/webhooks` — Create a webhook endpoint (returns secret once).

post.description:
Receives Telegram Bot API update POSTs.  Verified via the
`X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header that Telegram sends when a
`secret_token` was set in the `setWebhook` call.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/workflows` — Create a new workflow (server assigns the id).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}` — Fetch a single workflow's full definition.

put.summary: `PUT /api/v1/workflows/{id}` — Update an existing workflow in place.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/ws/ticket` — Mint a short-lived, single-use WebSocket auth
ticket carrying the presented key's scopes. Browsers can't send an
`Authorization` header on a WS upgrade; the ticket keeps the long-lived key
out of the URL query string (which lands in proxy logs and history).
post.description:
Not audit-logged: a ticket is a seconds-lived derivative of the presented
key with identical scopes, minted on every reconnect — logging each mint
would flood the audit trail with what is effectively "key was used".

fingerprint.description: First 16 hex chars of the public key, as a short fingerprint.

public_key_hex.description: Ed25519 public key (hex), if the agent has an identity.

supports_images.description: Whether the connected LLM adapter will emit native image blocks for this agent.

ApiChannelSummary.description: Summary of a connected bidirectional channel instance.

external_id.description: Channel-specific external identifier (chat id, topic, address).

health.description: Adapter health status, if a live adapter is running (`Healthy`, `Degraded`, ...).

ApiChatMessage.description:
A single chat message in a session timeline.

Maps from `ChatStore::get_messages()` → `ChatMessage`. Tool activity is
surfaced inline via the optional `tool_*` fields (the store joins
`chat_tool_calls` onto the owning message); user/assistant rows leave them
`None`. Note: per-message `tokens`/`cost`, multimodal `parts`, and the
reasoning `thinking` trace are not persisted in `chat_messages` and are
therefore intentionally absent from this DTO.

content.description: Message text (or `Tool call: <name>` for tool rows).

tool_duration_ms.description: Tool execution duration in milliseconds when `role == "tool"`.

tool_payload_json.description: Tool input payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

tool_result_json.description: Tool result payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

ApiChatSessionDetail.description: Detailed session view: header fields plus the full message timeline.

agent_name.description: Agent the session is bound to.

ApiChatSessionSummary.description:
Summary row for a chat session (list view).

Maps from `ChatStore::list_sessions()` → `ChatSession`. `preview` comes from
`ChatSession::last_preview` (last user/assistant message snippet).

agent_name.description: Agent the session is bound to.

message_count.description: Total number of messages in the session.

tools.description: Namespaced tool ids exposed by this connector (`<id>.<tool>`).

ApiConnectorSummary.description: Summary of a registered connector and its connection status.

connected.description: Whether a stored OAuth credential exists for this connector.

expires_at.description: Token expiry (from the stored credential), if known.

provider.description: OAuth provider name (from the stored credential), if connected.

ApiConvoDetail.description: Detailed conversation view: header fields plus the ordered turn list.

ApiConvoSummary.description:
Summary row for a multi-agent conversation (list view).

Maps from `ConvoStore::list_convos()` → `AgentConvo`.

ApiConvoTurn.description:
A single turn within a conversation.

Maps from `ConvoStore::get_turns()` → `ConvoTurn`.

ApiEscalation.description: A single escalation awaiting (or having received) a human decision.

ApiFileMeta.description:
Metadata for a single uploaded file. Mirrors
`agentos_kernel::file_store::UploadedFile` minus the on-disk path (never
exposed to clients).

id.description: Stable file id (UUID). Used in download/get/delete routes.

scope.description: `"global"` for ecosystem files, `"session:{id}"` for chat-scoped files.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached at upload time.

ApiKeyMeta.description: Management metadata for an API key (never includes key material).

ApiMcpServer.description: A running MCP server merged with its persisted attachment record.

created_at.description: When the attachment was persisted, if persisted.

note.description: Optional supervisor note (e.g. reconnect attempt count).

oauth_connector_id.description: OAuth connector id backing this server, if any.

state.description:
Live supervisor state (`Connected`, `Connecting`, `Backoff`, `Stopped`, ...).
`null` when only a persisted attachment exists with no live process.

oneOf.description: Call statistics (present only for live servers).

tool_count.description: Number of tools exposed by the server.

ApiMcpStats.description: Call statistics for a running MCP server.

ApiPluginSummary.description: Summary row for a discovered/active plugin.

channels.description: Channel ids declared by this plugin.

tools.description: Tool manifest paths declared by this plugin.

ApiPrefProposal.description: A single user-preference proposal awaiting (or having received) review.

ApiProposalStats.description: Aggregate counts across proposal lifecycle states.

proposed.description: Total proposals ever recorded (sum of all other counts).

ApiRole.description: A reusable role that groups a set of permissions.

permissions.description: Permissions in `"resource:rwxqo"` form (only the set flags are included).

ApiScheduleRun.description: A single recorded fire of a schedule (`GET /api/v1/schedules/{id}/runs`).

ApiScheduleSummary.description:
A single scheduled entry as returned by `GET /api/v1/schedules` — a
recurring cron job, a one-shot once-job, or an in-memory timer.

ApiScratchPage.description:
A full scratchpad page plus its backlinks. Mirrors `agentos_scratch::ScratchPage`
with the backlink summaries from `get_all_links`.

agent_id.description: Owning agent id (the reserved `__global__` sentinel for global pages).

backlinks.description: Pages that link to this page (backlink graph).

trigger_event_type.description:
Event type that triggered this task, if it was created by an event
subscription (e.g. `DiskSpaceLow`). Absent for user/scheduled tasks.

ApiWebhookEndpoint.description: A registered inbound webhook endpoint (no secret material).

id.description: Endpoint id (UUID) — used in the ingress URL.

inbound_url.description: Convenience: the ingress path for this endpoint.

status.description: Currently always `saved`; reserved for future lifecycle states.

AuthMe.description: Identity of the presented key — lets the SPA render scopes and gate UI.

ConfigTree.description: The full configuration tree, with secret-bearing leaves redacted.

config.description: The redacted config document as a JSON value.

pct.description: Percentage of the cost budget consumed.

oneOf.description: The active budget, when one is configured.

cost_pct.description: Percentage of the daily cost budget consumed (0 when unbudgeted).

forecast_exhaustion_hours.description: Linear forecast: estimated hours until the budget is exhausted.

tokens_pct.description: Percentage of the daily token budget consumed (0 when unbudgeted).

tool_calls_pct.description: Percentage of the daily tool-call budget consumed (0 when unbudgeted).

CreateChatSessionRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/chat/sessions`.

`first_message` is optional in the API contract, but the backing store only
exposes `create_session_with_first_message`. When omitted, the session is
created with an empty placeholder first message (see handler/kernel impl).

agent_name.description: Agent to bind the new session to.

first_message.description: Optional first user message. If omitted, an empty placeholder is stored.

title.description:
Optional initial title (currently informational — the store sets title to
NULL on create; rename via PATCH to set it).

CreateConvoRequest.description: Request body for creating + running a new multi-agent conversation.

max_turns.description: Number of turns to run (clamped to 2..=50; default 8).

CreateKeyRequest.description: Request to mint a new API key from the management surface (`keys:rw`).

name.description: Display name for the new key.

ttl_secs.description: Optional time-to-live in seconds. Omit for a non-expiring key.

delivery_mode.description: Accepted for forward-compatibility; v1 always creates Silent delivery.

CreateSubscriptionRequest.description: Create a new event subscription. Mirrors the kernel `event subscribe` command.

event_filter.description: Event filter: `all`, `category:<name>`, or an exact event type (`AgentAdded`).

CreateWebhookRequest.description: Create a new webhook endpoint for an agent.

count.description: Number of upcoming fire times to compute (default 5, capped at 50).

next_runs.description: RFC3339 timestamps of the next fire times.

detail.description: Detail message (and a suggested fix when applicable).

fixable.description: Whether `POST /api/v1/doctor/fix` can attempt to repair this check.

name.description: Human-readable check name, e.g. `"Config file exists"`.

check.description: Name of the check to attempt to fix. Empty/omitted = fix all fixable.

DoctorReport.description: Aggregate result of running all diagnostic checks.

all_ok.description: True when no check is in the `fail` state.

EmitEventRequest.description: Emit an event into the kernel event bus.

Envelope_ApiAgentDetail.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

Envelope_ApiAgentIdentity.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

data.description: Cryptographic identity of an agent (read-only).

fingerprint.description: First 16 hex chars of the public key, as a short fingerprint.

public_key_hex.description: Ed25519 public key (hex), if the agent has an identity.

Envelope_ApiAgentSummary.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

supports_images.description: Whether the connected LLM adapter will emit native image blocks for this agent.

Envelope_ApiChannelSummary.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

data.description: Summary of a connected bidirectional channel instance.

external_id.description: Channel-specific external identifier (chat id, topic, address).

health.description: Adapter health status, if a live adapter is running (`Healthy`, `Degraded`, ...).

data.description:
A single chat message in a session timeline.

Maps from `ChatStore::get_messages()` → `ChatMessage`. Tool activity is
surfaced inline via the optional `tool_*` fields (the store joins
`chat_tool_calls` onto the owning message); user/assistant rows leave them

tool_payload_json.description: Tool input payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

tool_result_json.description: Tool result payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description: Detailed session view: header fields plus the full message timeline.

agent_name.description: Agent the session is bound to.

Envelope_ApiConnectorDetail.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

data.description: Full connector detail.

tools.description: Namespaced tool ids exposed by this connector (`<id>.<tool>`).

Envelope_ApiConvoDetail.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description: Detailed conversation view: header fields plus the ordered turn list.

Envelope_ApiConvoSummary.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

data.description:
Summary row for a multi-agent conversation (list view).

Maps from `ConvoStore::list_convos()` → `AgentConvo`.

Envelope_ApiEscalation.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description: A single escalation awaiting (or having received) a human decision.

Envelope_ApiEventSubscription.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description: An agent's event subscription.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description:
Metadata for a single uploaded file. Mirrors

agent_id.description: Owning agent id (the reserved `__global__` sentinel for global pages).

Envelope_ApiTaskDetail.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

trigger_event_type.description:
Event type that triggered this task, if it was created by an event
subscription (e.g. `DiskSpaceLow`). Absent for user/scheduled tasks.

Envelope_ApiToolSummary.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

Envelope_ConfigTree.description:
Success envelope wrapping a response payload under a `data` key.

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or
`serde_json::Value` for ad-hoc acknowledgement bodies like `{ "ok": true }`).

Envelope_WsTicketResponse.description:

`T` is the inner payload type (a single DTO, a `Vec<DTO>` for lists, or

data.description:
A short-lived, single-use WebSocket auth ticket (`POST /api/v1/ws/ticket`).
Connect with `GET /api/v1/ws?ticket=…` before it expires.

ticket.description: Opaque single-use ticket; carries the minting key's scopes.

pending.description:
When `true`, return only pending (unresolved) escalations. When `false`
or omitted, return all escalations (including resolved).

title.description:
Optional title for the forked copy. When omitted, the store derives
`"<source title> (fork)"`.

ForkChatSessionResponse.description: Response body for a successful fork.

id.description: Id of the newly created forked session.

HalDevice.description: A registered HAL device and its per-agent access policy.

HalInfo.description: Hardware abstraction layer device inventory plus a system snapshot.

IssuedKeyResponse.description:
A newly minted API key, returned exactly once at login / key creation.
The raw `api_key` is never retrievable again.

api_key.description: The full `agos_<key>` secret — shown once; store it securely.

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp, if the key is time-limited.

key_id.description: The public, non-secret key id (use for `DELETE /keys/{id}`).

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to the key.

ListEnvelope_ApiChatMessage.description:
Success envelope for list endpoints that report pagination metadata.

Wraps the records under `data` and a [`Meta`] block under `meta`, i.e.
`{ "data": [...], "meta": { "total": N } }`.

items.description:
A single chat message in a session timeline.

Maps from `ChatStore::get_messages()` → `ChatMessage`. Tool activity is
surfaced inline via the optional `tool_*` fields (the store joins
`chat_tool_calls` onto the owning message); user/assistant rows leave them
`None`. Note: per-message `tokens`/`cost`, multimodal `parts`, and the
reasoning `thinking` trace are not persisted in `chat_messages` and are
therefore intentionally absent from this DTO.

content.description: Message text (or `Tool call: <name>` for tool rows).

tool_duration_ms.description: Tool execution duration in milliseconds when `role == "tool"`.

tool_payload_json.description: Tool input payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

tool_result_json.description: Tool result payload JSON string when `role == "tool"`.

data.description: The page of records.

ListEnvelope_ApiChatSessionSummary.description:
Success envelope for list endpoints that report pagination metadata.

Wraps the records under `data` and a [`Meta`] block under `meta`, i.e.
`{ "data": [...], "meta": { "total": N } }`.

items.description:
Summary row for a chat session (list view).

Maps from `ChatStore::list_sessions()` → `ChatSession`. `preview` comes from
`ChatSession::last_preview` (last user/assistant message snippet).

agent_name.description: Agent the session is bound to.

message_count.description: Total number of messages in the session.

data.description: The page of records.

ListEnvelope_ApiConvoSummary.description:
Success envelope for list endpoints that report pagination metadata.

Wraps the records under `data` and a [`Meta`] block under `meta`, i.e.
`{ "data": [...], "meta": { "total": N } }`.

items.description:
Summary row for a multi-agent conversation (list view).

Maps from `ConvoStore::list_convos()` → `AgentConvo`.

data.description: The page of records.

ListEnvelope_ApiFileMeta.description:
Success envelope for list endpoints that report pagination metadata.

Wraps the records under `data` and a [`Meta`] block under `meta`, i.e.
`{ "data": [...], "meta": { "total": N } }`.

items.description:
Metadata for a single uploaded file. Mirrors
`agentos_kernel::file_store::UploadedFile` minus the on-disk path (never
exposed to clients).

id.description: Stable file id (UUID). Used in download/get/delete routes.

scope.description: `"global"` for ecosystem files, `"session:{id}"` for chat-scoped files.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached at upload time.

data.description: The page of records.

ListEnvelope_ApiTaskSummary.description:
Success envelope for list endpoints that report pagination metadata.

Wraps the records under `data` and a [`Meta`] block under `meta`, i.e.
`{ "data": [...], "meta": { "total": N } }`.

data.description: The page of records.

line.description: The raw JSONL line as written to the audit log.

LoginRequest.description:
Operator login. The `credential` is verified (constant-time) against the
configured operator token (`[web] auth_token`); on success a scoped,
expiring API key is minted and returned once.

Meta.description: List metadata accompanying a paginated response.

total.description: Total number of records matching the query (before any limit/offset).

body.description: Truncated body text for list views.

limit.description: Maximum number of proposals to return (default 50).

status.description: Proposal status to filter by: `pending` (default), `accepted`, or `rejected`.

RenameChatSessionRequest.description:
Request body for `PATCH /api/v1/chat/sessions/{id}` (rename).

`title = null` clears the title.

title.description: New title, or `null` to clear it.

note.description: Optional free-form note recorded alongside the decision.

ResourceInfo.description: Host resource snapshot plus live resource-arbiter lock state.

contention.description: Contention statistics keyed by resource id.

SavePageRequest.description: Request body for `PUT /api/v1/scratchpad/{page}` (and the per-agent variant).

definition.description: Full workflow definition (nodes, connections, settings). Opaque to the API.

ScratchListResponse.description: Response wrapper for scratchpad list endpoints.

SendChatMessageRequest.description: Request body for sending a user message to a session (non-streaming).

value.description: The new value to write at the dotted key.

SubmitReviewRequest.description: Request body for submitting a marketplace review.

author_key.description: Public key id of the reviewer (non-secret).

WebhookSecretResponse.description: One-shot secret response, returned only on create/rotate.

secret.description: The HMAC secret — shown once; store it securely.

WorkflowSaveResponse.description: Response for workflow create/update — echoes the resolved id.

WsTicketResponse.description:
A short-lived, single-use WebSocket auth ticket (`POST /api/v1/ws/ticket`).
Connect with `GET /api/v1/ws?ticket=…` before it expires.

ticket.description: Opaque single-use ticket; carries the minting key's scopes.

bearer_auth.description: AgentOS API key presented as a Bearer token, e.g. `Authorization: Bearer agos_<key>`.

tags.name: chat-sessions
tags.description: Persisted chat-session management (list, create, rename, fork, export, messages)
